#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct UserData {
    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream(user_id, age, name);
    }
};

UserData deserialize_user_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer because the binary_stream requires a non-const buffer.
    // This is safe because we are only reading from the buffer during deserialization.

    // Create a binary_stream object from the non-const buffer.

    // Create a UserData object to hold the deserialized data.
    UserData data;

    // Use the deserialise method of the binary_stream to deserialize the UserData object.
    stream.deserialise(data);

    // Return the deserialized UserData object.
    return data;
}
```

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;
};

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer span
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>&>(buffer);

    // Create a buffer_adaptor from the non-const span
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Create a binary_stream from the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<std::span<uint8_t>> stream(adaptor);

    // Check if there is enough data for a PacketHeader
    if (stream.size() < sizeof(PacketHeader)) {
        return {};
    }

    // Use the binary_stream's span method to get a zero-copy view of the header
    auto header_span = stream.span<PacketHeader>(1);

    // If the span is empty (due to error), return default
    if (header_span.empty()) {
        return {};
    }

    // Return the first element of the span
    return header_span[0];
}
```

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor), hexi::allow_throw_t, hexi::endianness::little> stream(adaptor);

    stream << packet.header;
    stream << packet.data;
    stream.fill<16>(0x00);
    stream << packet.footer;

    return buffer;
}
```
